# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting and static reminders
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Analysis mode: topic-only | incremental | full-analytics
#   topic-only: Fast topic detection (~2s, minimal tokens)
#   incremental: Recent context analysis (~4s)
#   full-analytics: Comprehensive insights (~10s)
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
# If disabled, only cadence-based analysis will run
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Polling interval when transcript actively changing (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2

# Polling interval when transcript idle (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE=5

# Maximum sleeper runtime (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration regardless of clarity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Clarity score for sleeper to exit (1-10 scale)
# When clarity >= this value, sleeper hands off to fallback
SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
RESUME_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
     3→#
     4→# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
     5→# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
     6→#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
     7→#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
     8→#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
     9→#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
    10→#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
    11→#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
    12→#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
    13→#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
    14→#
    15→# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
    16→# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).
    17→
    18→# Double-source guard
    19→if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    20→    return 0
    21→fi
    22→readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1
    23→
    24→# Strict mode
    25→set -euo pipefail
    26→
    27→# ============================================================================
    28→# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
    29→# ============================================================================
    30→readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
    31→readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
    32→readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    33→readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
    34→readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    35→readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
    36→readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    37→readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
    38→readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
    39→readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'
    40→
    41→# ============================================================================
    42→# ERROR TRAP
    43→# ============================================================================
    44→error_trap() {
    45→    local line_number="$1"
    46→    local exit_code="$?"
    47→    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
    48→}
    49→trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR
    50→
    51→# ============================================================================
    52→# GLOBAL STATE
    53→# ============================================================================
    54→# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
    55→_LOG_FILE=""
    56→
    57→# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
    58→_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""
    59→
    60→# ============================================================================
    61→# LOGGING NAMESPACE
    62→# ============================================================================
    63→
    64→# Initialize session-specific logging
    65→# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
    66→# Args:
    67→#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
    68→# Globals:
    69→#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
    70→log_init() {
    71→    local session_id="$1"
    72→    local session_dir
    73→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    74→    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"
    75→
    76→    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    77→    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
    78→        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    79→    fi
    80→
    81→    # Log initialization
    82→    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
    83→}
    84→
    85→# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
    86→# Args:
    87→#   $* - message to log
    88→log_debug() {
    89→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    90→    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
    91→        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
    92→        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    93→    fi
    94→}
    95→
    96→# Log info message
    97→# Args:
    98→#   $* - message to log
    99→log_info() {
   100→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   101→    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
   102→        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
   103→        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
   104→    fi
   105→}
   106→
   107→# Log warning message
   108→# Args:
   109→#   $* - message to log
   110→log_warn() {
   111→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   112→    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
   113→        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
   114→        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
   115→    fi
   116→}
   117→
   118→# Log error message
   119→# Args:
   120→#   $* - message to log
   121→log_error() {
   122→    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
   123→    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
   124→}
   125→
   126→# Internal: Write log entry to file
   127→# Args:
   128→#   $1 - log level
   129→#   $2 - message
   130→_log_to_file() {
   131→    local level="$1"
   132→    shift
   133→    local message="$*"
   134→    local timestamp
   135→    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
   136→
   137→    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
   138→        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
   139→    fi
   140→}
   141→
   142→# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
   143→# Args:
   144→#   $1 - log level
   145→#   $2 - message
   146→_log_format_ansi() {
   147→    local level="$1"
   148→    shift
   149→    local message="$*"
   150→    local color=""
   151→
   152→    case "$level" in
   153→        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
   154→        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
   155→        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
   156→        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
   157→        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
   158→    esac
   159→
   160→    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
   161→}
   162→
   163→# ============================================================================
   164→# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
   165→# ============================================================================
   166→
   167→# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
   168→# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
   169→# Globals:
   170→#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
   171→config_load() {
   172→    local sidekick_root
   173→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   174→
   175→    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
   176→    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
   177→    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
   178→        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
   179→        exit 2
   180→    fi
   181→    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   182→    source "$defaults"
   183→
   184→    # 2. Load user config (optional)
   185→    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
   186→    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
   187→    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
   188→    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
   189→        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   190→        source "$user_config"
   191→    fi
   192→
   193→    # 3. Load project config (optional)
   194→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   195→        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   196→        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
   197→            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
   198→            source "$project_config"
   199→        fi
   200→    fi
   201→
   202→    # Validate configuration
   203→    _config_validate
   204→}
   205→
   206→# Get configuration value
   207→# Args:
   208→#   $1 - config key name
   209→# Returns:
   210→#   Config value or empty string
   211→config_get() {
   212→    local key="$1"
   213→    echo "${!key:-}"
   214→}
   215→
   216→# Check if feature is enabled
   217→# Args:
   218→#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
   219→# Returns:
   220→#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
   221→config_is_feature_enabled() {
   222→    local feature="$1"
   223→    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
   224→    local value="${!key:-false}"
   225→
   226→    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
   227→        return 0
   228→    else
   229→        return 1
   230→    fi
   231→}
   232→
   233→# Internal: Validate configuration values
   234→# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
   235→_config_validate() {
   236→    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
   237→    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
   238→    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
   239→        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
   240→        LOG_LEVEL="info"
   241→    fi
   242→
   243→    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
   244→    local numeric_vars=(
   245→        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
   246→        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
   247→        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
   248→        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
   249→        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
   250→        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
   251→        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
   252→        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
   253→        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
   254→        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
   255→        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
   256→        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
   257→        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
   258→        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
   259→    )
   260→
   261→    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
   262→        local value="${!var:-0}"
   263→        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
   264→            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
   265→            return 1
   266→        fi
   267→    done
   268→
   269→    return 0
   270→}
   271→
   272→# ============================================================================
   273→# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
   274→# ============================================================================
   275→
   276→# Detect execution scope (user or project)
   277→# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
   278→# Returns:
   279→#   "user" or "project"
   280→path_detect_scope() {
   281→    local script_path
   282→    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")
   283→
   284→    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
   285→        echo "user"
   286→    else
   287→        echo "project"
   288→    fi
   289→}
   290→
   291→# Get sidekick installation directory
   292→# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
   293→# Globals:
   294→#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
   295→path_get_sidekick_root() {
   296→    # Return cached value if available
   297→    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
   298→        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
   299→        return 0
   300→    fi
   301→
   302→    # Determine root from this script's location
   303→    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
   304→    local script_dir
   305→    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
   306→    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)
   307→
   308→    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
   309→}
   310→
   311→# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
   312→# Args:
   313→#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
   314→# Returns:
   315→#   Absolute path to session directory
   316→path_get_session_dir() {
   317→    local session_id="$1"
   318→    local sidekick_root
   319→    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
   320→    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"
   321→
   322→    # Create if doesn't exist
   323→    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   324→        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
   325→    fi
   326→
   327→    echo "$session_dir"
   328→}
   329→
   330→# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
   331→# Args:
   332→#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
   333→# Returns:
   334→#   Absolute path to project directory
   335→path_get_project_dir() {
   336→    local json="${1:-}"
   337→
   338→    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
   339→    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
   340→        local from_json
   341→        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
   342→        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
   343→            _path_normalize "$from_json"
   344→            return 0
   345→        fi
   346→    fi
   347→
   348→    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
   349→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
   350→        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   351→        return 0
   352→    fi
   353→
   354→    # Fall back to current directory
   355→    pwd
   356→}
   357→
   358→# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
   359→# Args:
   360→#   $1 - path to normalize
   361→# Returns:
   362→#   Normalized absolute path
   363→_path_normalize() {
   364→    local path="$1"
   365→
   366→    # Resolve to absolute path
   367→    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
   368→        readlink -f "$path"
   369→    else
   370→        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
   371→        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
   372→    fi
   373→}
   374→
   375→# ============================================================================
   376→# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
   377→# ============================================================================
   378→
   379→# Extract value from JSON using jq
   380→# Args:
   381→#   $1 - JSON string
   382→#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
   383→# Returns:
   384→#   Extracted value or empty string
   385→json_get() {
   386→    local json="$1"
   387→    local path="$2"
   388→
   389→    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
   390→}
   391→
   392→# Extract session_id from JSON
   393→# Args:
   394→#   $1 - JSON string
   395→# Returns:
   396→#   Session ID or empty string
   397→json_get_session_id() {
   398→    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
   399→}
   400→
   401→# Extract transcript_path from JSON
   402→# Args:
   403→#   $1 - JSON string
   404→# Returns:
   405→#   Transcript path or empty string
   406→json_get_transcript_path() {
   407→    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
   408→}
   409→
   410→# Validate JSON syntax
   411→# Args:
   412→#   $1 - JSON string
   413→# Returns:
   414→#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
   415→json_validate() {
   416→    local json="$1"
   417→    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
   418→}
   419→
   420→# Extract JSON from markdown code block
   421→# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
   422→# Args:
   423→#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
   424→# Returns:
   425→#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
   426→json_extract_from_markdown() {
   427→    local text="$1"
   428→
   429→    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
   430→    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
   431→        # Extract content between ```json and ```
   432→        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
   433→    else
   434→        # Return as-is
   435→        echo "$text"
   436→    fi
   437→}
   438→
   439→# ============================================================================
   440→# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
   441→# ============================================================================
   442→
   443→# Launch background process with PID tracking
   444→# Args:
   445→#   $1 - session_id
   446→#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
   447→#   $3 - function name to execute
   448→#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
   449→# Creates:
   450→#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
   451→#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
   452→process_launch_background() {
   453→    local session_id="$1"
   454→    local name="$2"
   455→    local function="$3"
   456→    shift 3
   457→    local args=("$@")
   458→
   459→    local session_dir
   460→    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
   461→    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
   462→    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"
   463→
   464→    # Check if already running
   465→    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   466→        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
   467→        return 0
   468→    fi
   469→
   470→    # Launch background process
   471→    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"
   472→
   473→    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
   474→    nohup bash -c "
   475→        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
   476→        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'
   477→
   478→        # Call the function with args
   479→        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
   480→    " &
   481→
   482→    local pid=$!
   483→    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
   484→
   485→    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
   486→}
   487→
   488→# Check if process is running by PID file
   489→# Args:
   490→#   $1 - PID file path
   491→# Returns:
   492→#   0 if running, 1 if not running
   493→process_is_running() {
   494→    local pid_file="$1"
   495→
   496→    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   497→        return 1
   498→    fi
   499→
   500→    local pid
   501→    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
   502→
   503→    # Check if PID exists and is running
   504→    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   505→        return 0
   506→    else
   507→        return 1
   508→    fi
   509→}
   510→
   511→# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
   512→# Args:
   513→#   $1 - PID file path
   514→process_kill() {
   515→    local pid_file="$1"
   516→
   517→    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   518→        return 0
   519→    fi
   520→
   521→    local pid
   522→    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")
   523→
   524→    # Try graceful shutdown first
   525→    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   526→        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
   527→        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   528→
   529→        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
   530→        local waited=0
   531→        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
   532→            sleep 1
   533→            waited=$((waited + 1))
   534→        done
   535→
   536→        # Force kill if still running
   537→        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
   538→            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
   539→            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   540→        fi
   541→    fi
   542→
   543→    # Remove PID file
   544→    rm -f "$pid_file"
   545→}
   546→
   547→# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
   548→# Args:
   549→#   $1 - session directory path
   550→process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
   551→    local session_dir="$1"
   552→
   553→    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
   554→        return 0
   555→    fi
   556→
   557→    # Find all .pid files
   558→    local pid_file
   559→    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
   560→        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
   561→            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
   562→                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
   563→                rm -f "$pid_file"
   564→            fi
   565→        fi
   566→    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
   567→}
   568→
   569→# ============================================================================
   570→# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
   571→# ============================================================================
   572→
   573→# Find Claude CLI binary
   574→# Returns:
   575→#   Path to claude binary
   576→# Exits:
   577→#   3 if claude binary not found
   578→claude_find_bin() {
   579→    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
   580→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
   581→        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
   582→        return 0
   583→    fi
   584→
   585→    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude
   586→    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
   587→    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
   588→        echo "$local_claude"
   589→        return 0
   590→    fi
   591→
   592→    # 3. Check PATH
   593→    if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
   594→        command -v claude
   595→        return 0
   596→    fi
   597→
   598→    # Not found
   599→    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
   600→    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
   601→    exit 3
   602→}
   603→
   604→# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
   605→# Args:
   606→#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
   607→#   $2 - prompt text
   608→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
   609→# Returns:
   610→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   611→# Exits:
   612→#   1 on error
   613→claude_invoke() {
   614→    local model="$1"
   615→    local prompt="$2"
   616→    local timeout="${3:-30}"
   617→
   618→    local claude_bin
   619→    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)
   620→
   621→    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   622→    local workspace
   623→    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")
   624→
   625→    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   626→
   627→    # Invoke Claude with timeout
   628→    local output
   629→    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
   630→        --model "$model" \
   631→        --workspace "$workspace" \
   632→        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
   633→        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
   634→        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   635→        return 1
   636→    fi
   637→
   638→    # Cleanup workspace
   639→    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   640→
   641→    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
   642→    local json
   643→    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")
   644→
   645→    # Validate JSON
   646→    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
   647→        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
   648→        return 1
   649→    fi
   650→
   651→    echo "$json"
   652→}
   653→
   654→# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
   655→# Args:
   656→#   $1 - Claude output text
   657→# Returns:
   658→#   Extracted JSON
   659→claude_extract_json() {
   660→    local output="$1"
   661→
   662→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
   663→    local extracted
   664→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")
   665→
   666→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
   667→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
   668→        echo "$extracted"
   669→        return 0
   670→    fi
   671→
   672→    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
   673→    # Match from first { to last }
   674→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
   675→        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
   676→    else
   677→        echo "$output"
   678→    fi
   679→}
   680→
   681→# ============================================================================
   682→# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
   683→# ============================================================================
   684→
   685→# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
   686→# Args:
   687→#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
   688→# Returns:
   689→#   Path to workspace directory
   690→workspace_create() {
   691→    local identifier="$1"
   692→    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"
   693→
   694→    # Create workspace directory
   695→    mkdir -p "$workspace"
   696→
   697→    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
   698→    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
   699→{
   700→  "hooks": {},
   701→  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
   702→}
   703→EOF
   704→
   705→    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
   706→    echo "$workspace"
   707→}
   708→
   709→# Cleanup workspace
   710→# Args:
   711→#   $1 - workspace path
   712→workspace_cleanup() {
   713→    local workspace="$1"
   714→
   715→    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
   716→        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
   717→        rm -rf "$workspace"
   718→    fi
   719→}
   720→
   721→# ============================================================================
   722→# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
   723→# ============================================================================
   724→
   725→# Validate that value is non-negative integer
   726→# Args:
   727→#   $1 - value to validate
   728→# Returns:
   729→#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
   730→util_validate_count() {
   731→    local value="$1"
   732→
   733→    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
   734→        return 0
   735→    else
   736→        return 1
   737→    fi
   738→}
   739→
   740→# Get file size (cross-platform)
   741→# Args:
   742→#   $1 - file path
   743→# Returns:
   744→#   File size in bytes
   745→util_get_file_size() {
   746→    local file="$1"
   747→
   748→    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
   749→        echo "0"
   750→        return 0
   751→    fi
   752→
   753→    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
   754→    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
   755→        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
   756→    else
   757→        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
   758→        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
   759→    fi
   760→}
   761→
   762→# Create session directory if missing
   763→# Args:
   764→#   $1 - session_id
   765→util_create_session_dir() {
   766→    local session_id="$1"
   767→    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
   768→}
   769→
   770→# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
   771→# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
   772→# Args:
   773→#   $1 - transcript file path
   774→# Returns:
   775→#   Estimated token count
   776→util_calculate_tokens() {
   777→    local transcript="$1"
   778→
   779→    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
   780→        echo "0"
   781→        return 0
   782→    fi
   783→
   784→    # Count characters and divide by 4
   785→    local chars
   786→    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   787→    echo $((chars / 4))
   788→}
   789→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize session-specific logging
# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Globals:
#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
log_init() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Log initialization
    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH
    if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        command -v claude
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"

    # Invoke Claude with timeout
    local output
    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        --workspace "$workspace" \
        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory
    mkdir -p "$workspace"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

```
~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/
```

Within each session directory (identified by a UUID session ID), you should see files like:

- **`sidekick.log`** - Main log file for the session
- **`response-count.txt`** - Response counter (if tracking is enabled)
- **`topic.json`** - Topic extraction results
- **`resume.txt`** - Resume information from previous sessions
- **`*.pid`** files - PID files for background processes (e.g., `sleeper.pid`)

#!/bin/bash
# Async LLM-based transcript analysis
# Analyzes conversation transcripts using Haiku models to extract topic, intent, and analytics
# Runs in detached background process to avoid blocking main conversation hooks

set -euo pipefail

# Script metadata
readonly SCRIPT_VERSION="1.0.0"
readonly SCRIPT_NAME="$(basename "$0")"

# Error trap for debugging
error_trap() {
    local exit_code=$?
    local line_no=$1
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FATAL] Script failed at line $line_no with exit code $exit_code" >> "${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/claude-analysis-error.log}" 2>&1 || true
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# Parse command-line arguments
usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: $SCRIPT_NAME <session_id> <transcript_path> <mode> [output_base_dir]

Arguments:
  session_id       UUID of the conversation session
  transcript_path  Path to JSONL transcript file
  mode             Analysis mode: topic-only|incremental|full-analytics
  output_base_dir  Base directory for output (default: <script_dir>)

Environment Variables:
  CLAUDE_ANALYSIS_MODEL    Model to use (default: haiku-4.5)
  CLAUDE_BIN               Path to Claude CLI binary (default: ~/.claude/local/claude)
  VERBOSE                  Enable verbose logging (default: false)

Output:
  Analysis files written to: <output_base_dir>/tmp/ or <output_base_dir>/analytics/

Example:
  $SCRIPT_NAME "abc-123" "/path/to/transcript.jsonl" "topic-only"
  $SCRIPT_NAME "abc-123" "/path/to/transcript.jsonl" "topic-only" "/project/.claude/hooks/reminders"
EOF
    exit 1
}

# Handle --help flag
if [ "${1:-}" = "--help" ] || [ "${1:-}" = "-h" ]; then
    usage
fi

# Validate arguments
[ $# -ge 3 ] || usage

session_id="$1"
transcript_path="$2"
mode="$3"
output_base_dir="${4:-}"  # Optional 4th parameter

# Configuration
CLAUDE_MODEL="${CLAUDE_ANALYSIS_MODEL:-haiku}"
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-false}"
DRY_RUN="${CLAUDE_ANALYSIS_DRY_RUN:-false}"

# Determine script directory and base directory EARLY (before any logging)
# This must happen before any log() or cleanup() calls since they reference these vars
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BASE_DIR="${output_base_dir:-$SCRIPT_DIR}"

# Create session-specific directory for logs and state files
SESSION_DIR="${BASE_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}"
mkdir -p "$SESSION_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true

# Set LOG_FILE and PID_FILE BEFORE any function calls that might reference them
LOG_FILE="${SESSION_DIR}/analysis.log"
PID_FILE="${SESSION_DIR}/analysis.pid"

# ANSI color codes for terminal output
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'

# Logging functions
log() {
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    if [ "$VERBOSE" = true ]; then
        echo -e "${COLOR_GREEN}[AnalyzeTranscript]${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    fi
    return 0
}

log_error() {
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [ERROR] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}[AnalyzeTranscript] ERROR:${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    return 0
}

log_warn() {
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [WARN] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    if [ "$VERBOSE" = true ]; then
        echo -e "${COLOR_YELLOW}[AnalyzeTranscript] WARN:${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    fi
    return 0
}

log_debug() {
    if [ "$VERBOSE" = true ]; then
        echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [DEBUG] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        echo -e "${COLOR_GRAY}[AnalyzeTranscript] DEBUG:${COLOR_RESET} $*" >&2
    fi
    return 0
}

# Cleanup function - called on exit
cleanup() {
    local exit_code=$?
    log_debug "Cleanup: removing workspace ${workspace_dir:-<unset>} and temp files"
    [ -n "${workspace_dir:-}" ] && [ -d "$workspace_dir" ] && rm -rf "$workspace_dir"
    [ -n "${simplified_transcript:-}" ] && [ -f "$simplified_transcript" ] && rm -f "$simplified_transcript"

    # Remove PID tracking file
    if [ -f "$PID_FILE" ]; then
        rm -f "$PID_FILE"
        log_debug "Removed PID file: $PID_FILE"
    fi

    log "Analysis completed with exit code: $exit_code"
    exit $exit_code
}
trap cleanup EXIT

# Validation
log "Starting analysis: session=$session_id, mode=$mode, model=$CLAUDE_MODEL"

if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
    log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
    exit 1
fi

# Validate mode
case "$mode" in
    topic-only|incremental|full-analytics)
        log_debug "Valid mode: $mode"
        ;;
    *)
        log_error "Invalid mode: $mode (must be topic-only, incremental, or full-analytics)"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

# Prompt template directory
PROMPT_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/analysis-prompts"

# Output directories:
# - Topic files always go to session-specific directory in tmp/
# - Full analytics files go to analytics/ directory for persistence
ANALYTICS_DIR="${BASE_DIR}/analytics"

if [ ! -d "$PROMPT_DIR" ]; then
    log_error "Prompt template directory not found: $PROMPT_DIR"
    exit 1
fi

# Ensure analytics directory exists if needed
if [ "$mode" = "full-analytics" ] && [ ! -d "$ANALYTICS_DIR" ]; then
    log_debug "Creating analytics directory: $ANALYTICS_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$ANALYTICS_DIR" || {
        log_error "Failed to create analytics directory: $ANALYTICS_DIR"
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Pre-process transcript to extract only message objects
# Converts raw JSONL format to simplified message-only JSONL
# Input: path to transcript file
# Output: path to simplified transcript file
preprocess_transcript() {
    local transcript_path="$1"
    local output_path="$2"

    log_debug "Pre-processing transcript: $transcript_path → $output_path"

    # Extract just the message object from each line
    # - Filter out null messages (lines without .message field)
    # - Filter out tool messages (where content[0].type is "tool_use" or "tool_result")
    # - Remove model, id, and type attributes to reduce size
    # - Write as compact single-line JSON
    jq -c '.message | select(. != null) | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end)) | del(.model, .id, .type)' "$transcript_path" > "$output_path" || {
        log_error "Failed to pre-process transcript"
        return 1
    }

    local line_count=$(wc -l < "$output_path")
    log_debug "Pre-processed $line_count message objects"

    return 0
}

# Extract relevant portion of transcript based on mode
# Now operates on pre-processed message-only transcript
extract_transcript_excerpt() {
    local mode="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    case "$mode" in
        topic-only)
            # Last 3 messages (approximately 500-1000 tokens)
            tail -n 80 "$transcript_path" | jq -s '.'
            ;;
        incremental)
            # Last 20 messages (approximately 2000-5000 tokens)
            tail -n 150 "$transcript_path" | jq -s '.'
            ;;
        full-analytics)
            # Full transcript
            jq -s '.' "$transcript_path"
            ;;
    esac
}

# Pre-process transcript to simplified format (extract message objects only)
simplified_transcript="/tmp/claude-messages-$$-${session_id}.jsonl"
log_debug "Pre-processing transcript to simplified format"
preprocess_transcript "$transcript_path" "$simplified_transcript" || {
    log_error "Failed to pre-process transcript"
    exit 1
}

log_debug "Extracting transcript excerpt for mode: $mode"
transcript_excerpt=$(extract_transcript_excerpt "$mode" "$simplified_transcript") || {
    log_error "Failed to extract transcript excerpt"
    exit 1
}

# Load prompt template
prompt_file="${PROMPT_DIR}/${mode}.txt"
if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
    log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
    exit 1
fi

log_debug "Loading prompt template: $prompt_file"
prompt_template=$(cat "$prompt_file")

# Substitute transcript excerpt into prompt
analysis_prompt="${prompt_template/\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_excerpt}"

# Create isolated workspace with empty hooks configuration
# This function ensures Claude runs without any hooks that could cause recursion
create_isolated_workspace() {
    local workspace_dir="$1"

    log_debug "Creating isolated workspace: $workspace_dir"

    mkdir -p "$workspace_dir/.claude" || {
        log_error "Failed to create workspace directory: $workspace_dir"
        return 1
    }

    # Write minimal settings.json with empty hooks object to prevent recursion
    # Also disable any other settings that might interfere
    cat > "$workspace_dir/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Workspace initialized with isolated settings"
    return 0
}

workspace_dir="/tmp/claude-analysis-$$-${session_id}"
create_isolated_workspace "$workspace_dir" || exit 1

# Execute claude -p with Haiku model in isolated workspace
log "Invoking Claude with model: $CLAUDE_MODEL"

cd "$workspace_dir" || {
    log_error "Failed to change to workspace directory"
    exit 1
}

# Capture both stdout and stderr
claude_output=$(mktemp)
claude_errors=$(mktemp)

# Determine Claude CLI path (handle alias or PATH-based installation)
CLAUDE_BIN="${CLAUDE_BIN:-$HOME/.claude/local/claude}"
if [ ! -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
    # Fallback: try to find in common locations
    for path in "$HOME/.claude/local/claude" "$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null)"; do
        if [ -x "$path" ]; then
            CLAUDE_BIN="$path"
            break
        fi
    done
fi

if [ ! -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
    log_error "Claude CLI not found. Set CLAUDE_BIN environment variable or ensure claude is in PATH"
    exit 1
fi

log_debug "Using Claude binary: $CLAUDE_BIN"

# DRY RUN MODE: Generate mock JSON output for testing
if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
    log "DRY RUN MODE: Generating mock JSON output"
    cat > "$claude_output" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "timestamp": "$(date -Iseconds)",
  "task_ids": ["DRY-RUN"],
  "initial_goal": "Testing analyze-transcript.sh",
  "current_objective": "Verify dry-run mode",
  "clarity_score": 10,
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "high_clarity_snarky_comment": "Dry runs are for cowards who fear actual LLM calls",
  "low_clarity_snarky_comment": "Even in testing, confusion reigns supreme"
}
EOF
    exit_code=0
else
    # Run claude -p (project mode) with JSON output format
    # Use timeout to prevent hanging (30s should be plenty for topic-only)
    # Exit code 124 = timeout, others = claude errors
    # Temporarily disable set -e to capture exit code
    set +e
    timeout 30s "$CLAUDE_BIN" -p --model "$CLAUDE_MODEL" --setting-sources project <<EOF > "$claude_output" 2> "$claude_errors"
$analysis_prompt
EOF

    exit_code=$?
    set -e
fi

if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
    if [ $exit_code -eq 124 ]; then
        log_error "Claude execution timed out after 30 seconds"
    else
        log_error "Claude execution failed (exit code: $exit_code)"
    fi

    # Log both stdout and stderr for debugging
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_error "=== Claude stderr ==="
        log_error "$(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    if [ -s "$claude_output" ]; then
        log_error "=== Claude stdout (first 50 lines) ==="
        log_error "$(head -50 "$claude_output")"
    fi

    rm -f "$claude_output" "$claude_errors"
    exit 1
fi

# Check for stderr warnings/errors
if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
    log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
fi

# Log raw Claude response before parsing
log_debug "Raw Claude response:"
log_debug "$(cat "$claude_output")"

# Extract JSON from Claude output
# Claude may return markdown-wrapped JSON, so extract code block if present
log_debug "Parsing Claude output for JSON"

# Try to extract JSON from markdown code block first
json_output=$(sed -n '/```json/,/```/p' "$claude_output" | sed '1d;$d')

# If no markdown block, assume raw JSON
if [ -z "$json_output" ]; then
    json_output=$(cat "$claude_output")
fi

# Validate JSON
if ! echo "$json_output" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
    log_error "Invalid JSON output from Claude"
    log_error "Raw output: $(cat "$claude_output")"
    rm -f "$claude_output" "$claude_errors"
    exit 1
fi

log "Successfully parsed JSON output"

# Always write topic file to session-specific directory
topic_file="${SESSION_DIR}/topic.json"
echo "$json_output" | jq '.' > "$topic_file" || {
    log_error "Failed to write topic file: $topic_file"
    rm -f "$claude_output" "$claude_errors"
    exit 1
}

log "Wrote topic analysis: $topic_file"

# For full analytics mode, also write separate analytics file
if [ "$mode" = "full-analytics" ]; then
    analytics_file="${ANALYTICS_DIR}/${session_id}_analytics.json"
    echo "$json_output" | jq '.' > "$analytics_file" || {
        log_error "Failed to write analytics file: $analytics_file"
        rm -f "$claude_output" "$claude_errors"
        exit 1
    }
    log "Wrote full analytics: $analytics_file"
fi

# Cleanup temp files
rm -f "$claude_output" "$claude_errors"

# Extract key metrics for logging
primary_topic=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.primary_topic // "unknown"')
clarity_score=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 0')
log "Analysis complete: topic='$primary_topic', clarity=$clarity_score"

exit 0

Perfect! Now I can see the legacy pattern clearly:

1. **Isolated workspace creation** (lines 252-275):
   - Creates a temp directory (e.g., `/tmp/claude-analysis-$$-${session_id}`)
   - Creates `.claude/settings.json` with empty hooks and disabled statusline
   - Uses `--setting-sources project` flag with `claude -p` (line 334)

2. **Claude binary detection** (lines 293-309):
   - Checks `CLAUDE_BIN` env var first
   - Defaults to `~/.claude/local/claude`
   - Falls back to `command -v claude`

3. **Session directory** (line 70):
   - `${BASE_DIR}/tmp/${session_id}` in the legacy code

So for the fixes:

**Fix 1: Hook recursion** - Update `claude_invoke()` in `src/sidekick/lib/common.sh` to:
- Use the `create_isolated_workspace()` pattern
- Call `cd "$workspace_dir"` before invoking claude
- Use `claude -p --setting-sources project` instead of the non-existent `--workspace` flag

**Fix 2: Session files location** - Change from:
- User scope: `~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/`
- Project scope: `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/`

To:
- User scope: `~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/` (unchanged - still makes sense in user scope)
- Project scope: `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/` (visible in workspace)

Actually, wait. Let me re-read Scott's request... he said:

"I suggest a project-local .sidekick/ folder that has in it the sessions folders containing the state and logs."


I'll outline a clear strategy for implementing the session directory structure. The project-local approach requires creating a `.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/` directory at the project root, while maintaining the existing user-scope directory. This ensures clean separation between project-specific and user-wide session management. I'll draft a systematic approach to implement this new directory structure, focusing on maintaining existing functionality while providing a more intuitive project-local session storage mechanism.

content.text:
Good catch! I see two clear issues:

1. **Hook recursion bug**: Using non-existent `--workspace` flag instead of the legacy pattern with `--setting-sources project`
2. **Session files visibility**: In project scope, files should be at `.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/` instead of `.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/`

I agree with your proposed location. For **user scope**, keeping `~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/` makes sense. For **project scope**, `.sidekick/sessions/` at the project root keeps state visible and separate from the installation directory.

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"

    # Invoke Claude with timeout
    local output
    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        --workspace "$workspace" \
        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt and capture output
    # shellcheck disable=SC2030
    if ! output=$(cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project 2>"$claude_errors" <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ); then
        local exit_code=$?
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code}): ${errors}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}
