#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize session-specific logging
# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Globals:
#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
log_init() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Log initialization
    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH
    if command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        command -v claude
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"

    # Invoke Claude with timeout
    local output
    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        --workspace "$workspace" \
        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory
    mkdir -p "$workspace"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

toolUseResult.newString:
# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt and capture output
    # shellcheck disable=SC2030
    if ! output=$(cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project 2>"$claude_errors" <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ); then
        local exit_code=$?
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code}): ${errors}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize session-specific logging
# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Globals:
#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
log_init() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Log initialization
    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"

    # Invoke Claude with timeout
    local output
    if ! output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        --workspace "$workspace" \
        --message "$prompt" 2>&1); then
        log_error "Claude invocation failed: $output"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory
    mkdir -p "$workspace"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)

# Returns:

workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled

#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize session-specific logging
# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Globals:
#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
log_init() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Log initialization
    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt and capture output
    # shellcheck disable=SC2030
    if ! output=$(cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project 2>"$claude_errors" <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ); then
        local exit_code=$?
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code}): ${errors}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory
    mkdir -p "$workspace"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude.settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

input.new_string:
# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
# Notes:
#   - User scope: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/
#   - Project scope: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local scope
    scope=$(path_detect_scope)
    local session_dir

    if [ "$scope" = "project" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        # Project scope: use visible .sidekick/sessions/ directory at project root
        session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
    else
        # User scope: use sidekick installation tmp/ directory
        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
        session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"
    fi

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}
