#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Handler: UserPromptSubmit
# Orchestrates features for user prompt submission

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_HANDLER_USER_PROMPT_SUBMIT_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_HANDLER_USER_PROMPT_SUBMIT_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before user-prompt-submit.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# handler_user_prompt_submit - Handle UserPromptSubmit hook event
#
# Orchestrates user prompt submission:
# - Increments tracking counter
# - Launches sleeper on first call (if topic extraction enabled)
# - Checks cadence-based analysis (if topic extraction enabled)
# - Checks static reminder cadence
# - Outputs hook JSON if reminder is due
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, non-zero on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
handler_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "handler_user_prompt_submit: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    if [ -z "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "handler_user_prompt_submit: transcript_path is required"
        return 1
    fi

    if [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "handler_user_prompt_submit: project_dir is required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "UserPromptSubmit handler: session=$session_id, transcript=$transcript_path"

    # Get sidekick root for sourcing features
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local count=0

    # Source and increment tracking feature
    if config_is_feature_enabled "tracking"; then
        log_debug "Sourcing tracking feature"
        # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/tracking.sh
        source "$sidekick_root/features/tracking.sh" || {
            log_error "Failed to source tracking feature"
            return 1
        }

        log_debug "Incrementing tracking counter"
        count=$(tracking_increment "$session_id")
        if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            log_error "Failed to increment tracking counter"
            return 1
        fi
        log_debug "Current count: $count"
    else
        log_debug "Tracking feature is disabled"
    fi

    # Handle topic extraction feature (sleeper + cadence analysis)
    if config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction is enabled"

        # Check if topic-extraction.sh exists before sourcing
        if [ -f "$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh" ]; then
            log_debug "Sourcing topic-extraction feature"
            # shellcheck source=src/sidekick/features/topic-extraction.sh
            source "$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh" || {
                log_error "Failed to source topic-extraction feature"
                return 1
            }

            # Launch sleeper on first call (count == 1)
            if [ "$count" -eq 1 ]; then
                log_debug "First call - launching sleeper"
                if ! topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
                    log_warn "Failed to launch sleeper (non-fatal)"
                fi
            else
                log_debug "Not first call (count=$count) - skipping sleeper launch"
            fi

            # Check cadence-based analysis (fallback)
            log_debug "Checking cadence-based analysis"
            if ! topic_extraction_check_cadence "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir" "$count"; then
                log_warn "Cadence-based analysis failed (non-fatal)"
            fi
        else
            log_warn "topic-extraction.sh not found (feature may not be implemented yet)"
        fi
    else
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature is disabled"
    fi

    # Check static reminder cadence
    local reminder_text=""
    if config_is_feature_enabled "tracking"; then
        log_debug "Checking static reminder cadence"
        reminder_text=$(tracking_check_reminder "$count" "$project_dir")
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            log_info "Static reminder due at count $count"
            # Escape reminder text for JSON (replace newlines with \n, escape quotes)
            local escaped_reminder
            escaped_reminder=$(echo "$reminder_text" | jq -Rs .)
            # Output hook JSON with additionalContext
            cat <<JSON
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
    "additionalContext": $escaped_reminder
  }
}
JSON
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder due at count $count"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "UserPromptSubmit handler completed successfully"
    return 0
}

log_debug "UserPromptSubmit handler loaded"

# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting and static reminders
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Analysis mode: topic-only | incremental | full-analytics
#   topic-only: Fast topic detection (~2s, minimal tokens)
#   incremental: Recent context analysis (~4s)
#   full-analytics: Comprehensive insights (~10s)
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
# If disabled, only cadence-based analysis will run
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Polling interval when transcript actively changing (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2

# Polling interval when transcript idle (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE=5

# Maximum sleeper runtime (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration regardless of clarity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Clarity score for sleeper to exit (1-10 scale)
# When clarity >= this value, sleeper hands off to fallback
SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
RESUME_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

file.content:
# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting and static reminders
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Analysis mode: topic-only | incremental | full-analytics
#   topic-only: Fast topic detection (~2s, minimal tokens)
#   incremental: Recent context analysis (~4s)
#   full-analytics: Comprehensive insights (~10s)
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
# If disabled, only cadence-based analysis will run
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Polling interval when transcript actively changing (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2

# Polling interval when transcript idle (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE=5

# Maximum sleeper runtime (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration regardless of clarity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Clarity score for sleeper to exit (1-10 scale)
# When clarity >= this value, sleeper hands off to fallback
SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
RESUME_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
_SIDEKICK_ROOT=""

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize session-specific logging
# Creates log file in tmp/${session_id}/sidekick.log
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Globals:
#   _LOG_FILE - set to log file path
log_init() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

    # Create log file if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        touch "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Log initialization
    _log_to_file "INFO" "=== Sidekick session started: ${session_id} ==="
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')

    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory
# Notes:
#   - User scope: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/tmp/{session-id}/
#   - Project scope: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local scope
    scope=$(path_detect_scope)
    local session_dir

    if [ "$scope" = "project" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        # Project scope: use visible .sidekick/sessions/ directory at project root
        session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
    else
        # User scope: use sidekick installation tmp/ directory
        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
        session_dir="${sidekick_root}/tmp/${session_id}"
    fi

    # Create if doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"
    fi

    echo "$session_dir"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   tmp/${session_id}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt and capture output
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    output=$(cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project 2>"$claude_errors" <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    )
    exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code}): ${errors}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    path_get_session_dir "$session_id" >/dev/null
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf

#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting and static reminders
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Analysis mode: topic-only | incremental | full-analytics
#   topic-only: Fast topic detection (~2s, minimal tokens)
#   incremental: Recent context analysis (~4s)
#   full-analytics: Comprehensive insights (~10s)
TOPIC_MODE=topic-only

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
# If disabled, only cadence-based analysis will run
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Polling interval when transcript actively changing (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE=2

# Polling interval when transcript idle (seconds)
SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE=5

# Maximum sleeper runtime (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration regardless of clarity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Clarity score for sleeper to exit (1-10 scale)
# When clarity >= this value, sleeper hands off to fallback
SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT=7

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku-4.5, haiku-3.5, haiku-3
RESUME_MODEL=haiku-4.5

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=debug

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

input.prompt: What are the requirements for UserPromptSubmit hooks to fire automatically? Are there any permissions, settings, or conditions needed? How does it differ from SessionStart hooks?

content.content:
# UserPromptSubmit Hook Requirements

## Automatic Firing

UserPromptSubmit hooks fire automatically when "the user submits a prompt, before Claude processes it." There are no special permissions or settings required—they execute as part of the normal prompt submission workflow.

## Key Characteristics

**UserPromptSubmit** allows you to:
- Validate prompts before processing
- Block certain types of prompts
- "Add additional context based on the prompt/conversation"

## Differences from SessionStart

| Aspect | UserPromptSubmit | SessionStart |
|--------|-----------------|--------------|
| **Timing** | Each time user submits a prompt | Once at session start |
| **Purpose** | Per-prompt validation/enrichment | One-time context loading |
| **Context Access** | Current prompt text | Entire session state |
| **Output Handling** | Stdout injected as context (exit 0) | Stdout added as context |
| **Blocking** | Can block individual prompts | Cannot block session |

UserPromptSubmit is granular and recurring, firing on every submission. SessionStart is a one-time initialization hook executed when Claude Code "starts a new session or resumes an existing session."

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# sidekick.sh - Main entry point for Sidekick hooks system
     3→#
     4→# Routes hook events to appropriate handlers and features.
     5→#
     6→# Commands:
     7→#   sidekick.sh session-start      - SessionStart hook
     8→#   sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit - UserPromptSubmit hook
     9→#   sidekick.sh statusline         - Statusline rendering
    10→#
    11→# Hook commands receive JSON via stdin containing session_id, workspace, etc.
    12→# Statusline command receives JSON via stdin containing session metadata.
    13→
    14→set -euo pipefail
    15→
    16→# ============================================================================
    17→# BOOTSTRAP
    18→# ============================================================================
    19→
    20→# Get directory where this script lives
    21→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    22→
    23→# Source shared library (loads all functions)
    24→# shellcheck disable=SC1091
    25→source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/lib/common.sh"
    26→
    27→# ============================================================================
    28→# MAIN EXECUTION
    29→# ============================================================================
    30→
    31→main() {
    32→    local command="${1:-}"
    33→
    34→    # Validate command provided
    35→    if [ -z "$command" ]; then
    36→        usage
    37→        exit 1
    38→    fi
    39→
    40→    # Load configuration cascade
    41→    config_load
    42→
    43→    # Route to appropriate handler based on command
    44→    case "$command" in
    45→        session-start)
    46→            handle_session_start
    47→            ;;
    48→        user-prompt-submit)
    49→            handle_user_prompt_submit
    50→            ;;
    51→        statusline)
    52→            handle_statusline
    53→            ;;
    54→        --version|-v)
    55→            show_version
    56→            ;;
    57→        --help|-h)
    58→            usage
    59→            ;;
    60→        *)
    61→            echo "ERROR: Unknown command: ${command}" >&2
    62→            usage
    63→            exit 1
    64→            ;;
    65→    esac
    66→}
    67→
    68→# ============================================================================
    69→# HANDLERS
    70→# ============================================================================
    71→
    72→# Handle SessionStart hook
    73→handle_session_start() {
    74→    # Read JSON input from stdin
    75→    local input
    76→    input=$(cat)
    77→
    78→    # Validate JSON
    79→    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
    80→        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
    81→        exit 1
    82→    fi
    83→
    84→    # Extract session_id
    85→    local session_id
    86→    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
    87→    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
    88→        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
    89→        exit 1
    90→    fi
    91→
    92→    # Extract project directory
    93→    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    94→    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")
    95→
    96→    # Initialize logging
    97→    log_init "$session_id"
    98→    log_info "SessionStart hook invoked for session ${session_id}"
    99→
   100→    # Source and execute handler
   101→    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
   102→    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/session-start.sh"
   103→    handler_session_start "$session_id" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   104→
   105→    log_info "SessionStart hook completed"
   106→}
   107→
   108→# Handle UserPromptSubmit hook
   109→handle_user_prompt_submit() {
   110→    # Read JSON input from stdin
   111→    local input
   112→    input=$(cat)
   113→
   114→    # Validate JSON
   115→    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
   116→        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
   117→        exit 1
   118→    fi
   119→
   120→    # Extract session_id
   121→    local session_id
   122→    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
   123→    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
   124→        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
   125→        exit 1
   126→    fi
   127→
   128→    # Extract transcript path
   129→    local transcript_path
   130→    transcript_path=$(json_get_transcript_path "$input")
   131→    if [ -z "$transcript_path" ]; then
   132→        echo "ERROR: Missing transcript_path in input" >&2
   133→        exit 1
   134→    fi
   135→
   136→    # Extract project directory
   137→    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
   138→    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")
   139→
   140→    # Initialize logging
   141→    log_init "$session_id"
   142→    log_debug "UserPromptSubmit hook invoked for session ${session_id}"
   143→
   144→    # Source and execute handler
   145→    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
   146→    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/user-prompt-submit.sh"
   147→    handler_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   148→
   149→    log_debug "UserPromptSubmit hook completed"
   150→}
   151→
   152→# Handle statusline rendering
   153→handle_statusline() {
   154→    # Read JSON input from stdin
   155→    local input
   156→    input=$(cat)
   157→
   158→    # Validate JSON
   159→    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
   160→        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
   161→        exit 1
   162→    fi
   163→
   164→    # Extract session_id
   165→    local session_id
   166→    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
   167→
   168→    # Extract project directory from input or args
   169→    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
   170→    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# sidekick.sh - Main entry point for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Routes hook events to appropriate handlers and features.
#
# Commands:
#   sidekick.sh session-start      - SessionStart hook
#   sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit - UserPromptSubmit hook
#   sidekick.sh statusline         - Statusline rendering
#
# Hook commands receive JSON via stdin containing session_id, workspace, etc.
# Statusline command receives JSON via stdin containing session metadata.

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# BOOTSTRAP
# ============================================================================

# Get directory where this script lives
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# Source shared library (loads all functions)
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION
# ============================================================================

main() {
    local command="${1:-}"

    # Validate command provided
    if [ -z "$command" ]; then
        usage
        exit 1
    fi

    # Load configuration cascade
    config_load

    # Route to appropriate handler based on command
    case "$command" in
        session-start)
            handle_session_start
            ;;
        user-prompt-submit)
            handle_user_prompt_submit
            ;;
        statusline)
            handle_statusline
            ;;
        --version|-v)
            show_version
            ;;
        --help|-h)
            usage
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown command: ${command}" >&2
            usage
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# HANDLERS
# ============================================================================

# Handle SessionStart hook
handle_session_start() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract project directory
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # Initialize logging
    log_init "$session_id"
    log_info "SessionStart hook invoked for session ${session_id}"

    # Source and execute handler
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/session-start.sh"
    handler_session_start "$session_id" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

    log_info "SessionStart hook completed"
}

# Handle UserPromptSubmit hook
handle_user_prompt_submit() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract transcript path
    local transcript_path
    transcript_path=$(json_get_transcript_path "$input")
    if [ -z "$transcript_path" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing transcript_path in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract project directory
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # Initialize logging
    log_init "$session_id"
    log_debug "UserPromptSubmit hook invoked for session ${session_id}"

    # Source and execute handler
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/user-prompt-submit.sh"
    handler_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

    log_debug "UserPromptSubmit hook completed"
}

# Handle statusline rendering
handle_statusline() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")

    # Extract project directory from input or args
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # If session_id available, initialize logging
    # (statusline may be called without session_id during startup)
    if [ -n "$session_id" ]; then
        log_init "$session_id"
    fi

    # Source and execute feature
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/features/statusline.sh"
    feature_statusline_render "$input"
}

# ============================================================================
# HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Show version information
show_version() {
    echo "Sidekick v1.0.0"
    echo "Modular hooks system for Claude Code"
}

# Show usage information
usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: sidekick.sh <command>

Commands:
  session-start       SessionStart hook (reads JSON from stdin)
  user-prompt-submit  UserPromptSubmit hook (reads JSON from stdin)
  statusline          Statusline rendering (reads JSON from stdin)
  --version, -v       Show version information
  --help, -h          Show this help message

Examples:
  # SessionStart hook
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123"}' | sidekick.sh session-start

  # UserPromptSubmit hook
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123","transcript_path":"/path/to/transcript.jsonl"}' | sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit

  # Statusline rendering
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123","model":{"display_name":"Sonnet 4.5"}}' | sidekick.sh statusline

Configuration:
  Default config: ${SCRIPT_DIR}/config.defaults
  User config:    ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
  Project config: \$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf

For more information, see: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code
EOF
}

# ============================================================================
# ENTRY POINT
# ============================================================================

main "$@"

content.thinking:
I need to add debug logging at the very top of sidekick.sh, before any processing happens. This should log:
1. The command being executed
2. All arguments
3. Whether stdin has data
4. The raw stdin content

The challenge is that we need to log before config_load is called (which happens in main()), but we also need to be able to write to a debug log file. 

Let me add logging right after the script directory is determined but before anything else. I'll write to a global debug log file at /tmp/sidekick-debug.log that's always available regardless of session.

#!/bin/bash
# sidekick.sh - Main entry point for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Routes hook events to appropriate handlers and features.
#
# Commands:
#   sidekick.sh session-start      - SessionStart hook
#   sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit - UserPromptSubmit hook
#   sidekick.sh statusline         - Statusline rendering
#
# Hook commands receive JSON via stdin containing session_id, workspace, etc.
# Statusline command receives JSON via stdin containing session metadata.

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# EARLY DEBUG LOGGING
# ============================================================================

# Debug log file (always enabled for diagnosing hook invocation issues)
SIDEKICK_DEBUG_LOG="/tmp/sidekick-debug.log"

# Log invocation details immediately (before any processing)
{
    echo "=============================================="
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] Sidekick invoked"
    echo "Command: ${1:-<none>}"
    echo "Args: $*"
    echo "PWD: $PWD"
    echo "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-<not set>}"
    echo "Has stdin: $([ -t 0 ] && echo 'no (TTY)' || echo 'yes (pipe)')"
    if [ ! -t 0 ]; then
        echo "Reading stdin..."
    fi
} >> "$SIDEKICK_DEBUG_LOG" 2>&1

# ============================================================================
# BOOTSTRAP
# ============================================================================

# Get directory where this script lives
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

# Source shared library (loads all functions)
# shellcheck disable=SC1091
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION
# ============================================================================

main() {
    local command="${1:-}"

    # Validate command provided
    if [ -z "$command" ]; then
        usage
        exit 1
    fi

    # Load configuration cascade
    config_load

    # Route to appropriate handler based on command
    case "$command" in
        session-start)
            handle_session_start
            ;;
        user-prompt-submit)
            handle_user_prompt_submit
            ;;
        statusline)
            handle_statusline
            ;;
        --version|-v)
            show_version
            ;;
        --help|-h)
            usage
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown command: ${command}" >&2
            usage
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# HANDLERS
# ============================================================================

# Handle SessionStart hook
handle_session_start() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Log input to debug file
    {
        echo "SessionStart input received:"
        echo "$input" | jq '.' 2>&1 || echo "$input"
    } >> "$SIDEKICK_DEBUG_LOG" 2>&1

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract project directory
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # Initialize logging
    log_init "$session_id"
    log_info "SessionStart hook invoked for session ${session_id}"

    # Source and execute handler
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/session-start.sh"
    handler_session_start "$session_id" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

    log_info "SessionStart hook completed"
}

# Handle UserPromptSubmit hook
handle_user_prompt_submit() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")
    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing session_id in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract transcript path
    local transcript_path
    transcript_path=$(json_get_transcript_path "$input")
    if [ -z "$transcript_path" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Missing transcript_path in input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract project directory
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # Initialize logging
    log_init "$session_id"
    log_debug "UserPromptSubmit hook invoked for session ${session_id}"

    # Source and execute handler
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/handlers/user-prompt-submit.sh"
    handler_user_prompt_submit "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

    log_debug "UserPromptSubmit hook completed"
}

# Handle statusline rendering
handle_statusline() {
    # Read JSON input from stdin
    local input
    input=$(cat)

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$input"; then
        echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON input" >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Extract session_id
    local session_id
    session_id=$(json_get_session_id "$input")

    # Extract project directory from input or args
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR
    CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR=$(path_get_project_dir "$input")

    # If session_id available, initialize logging
    # (statusline may be called without session_id during startup)
    if [ -n "$session_id" ]; then
        log_init "$session_id"
    fi

    # Source and execute feature
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/features/statusline.sh"
    feature_statusline_render "$input"
}

# ============================================================================
# HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Show version information
show_version() {
    echo "Sidekick v1.0.0"
    echo "Modular hooks system for Claude Code"
}

# Show usage information
usage() {
    cat <<EOF
Usage: sidekick.sh <command>

Commands:
  session-start       SessionStart hook (reads JSON from stdin)
  user-prompt-submit  UserPromptSubmit hook (reads JSON from stdin)
  statusline          Statusline rendering (reads JSON from stdin)
  --version, -v       Show version information
  --help, -h          Show this help message

Examples:
  # SessionStart hook
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123"}' | sidekick.sh session-start

  # UserPromptSubmit hook
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123","transcript_path":"/path/to/transcript.jsonl"}' | sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit

  # Statusline rendering
  echo '{"session_id":"abc-123","model":{"display_name":"Sonnet 4.5"}}' | sidekick.sh statusline

Configuration:
  Default config: ${SCRIPT_DIR}/config.defaults
  User config:    ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
  Project config: \$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf

For more information, see: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code
EOF
}

# ============================================================================
# ENTRY POINT
# ============================================================================

main "$@"
